meaning: vt.使变得敏感,使具有感光性vi.变得敏感,敏化
examples: It seems important to sensitize people to the fact that depression is more than the blues.
The allergen in jackfruit does not sensitize the immune system, but once birch pollen has done so, the immune system may react to jackfruit too.
The purpose of this essay is to sensitize Western audience to the perceptions of the rest of the world.
They were expected to help sensitize and develop larger ownership of community regarding importance of education in their areas.
More female directors may sensitize boards to CSR initiatives and provide perspectives that can be helpful in addressing key issues.
